extraction.one_line_summary: CHAVEZ Esteban won Stage 19 with repeated attacks in final 8km, finally breaking clear at 2.5km after multiple accelerations cracked his companions.
tactical_patterns: CHAVEZ attacked repeatedly without creating gap until competitors cracked psychologically, not physiologically—persistence over single explosive move won.
CARAPAZ shadowed ROGLIC's late finishing attacks immediately, showing GC leader defending by wheel-following rather than maintaining tempo.
LOPEZ attacked 7.9km out despite being down in GC, recouping significant time by going early when favorites still controlling.
LANDA mechanical at critical moment reveals Trek-Segafredo's vulnerability to equipment failure on decisive climbs with no teammates for bike changes.

rider_signals.CHAVEZ Esteban: positive: multiple failed attacks didn't deter continued aggression—mental resilience indicator for future breakaway selections
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: neutral: attempted late finishing attacks for time splits but CARAPAZ immediately marked every move, showing neither weakness nor dominance
rider_signals.LOPEZ Miguel Angel: positive: attacked 7.9km out while lower in GC and still gained nearly 1 minute, suggesting willingness to gamble early pays dividends
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: negative: mechanical at worst moment with no team support nearby exposes Trek-Segafredo's poor positioning in critical finale
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: defended pink jersey by immediate reaction to ROGLIC rather than setting tempo, showing tactical flexibility under pressure
